stories.title: ServiceNow bets $40 million on Indian banking software specialist to expand its financial services push
stories.simple_summary: ServiceNow is giving $40 million to help an Indian company that makes banking software with AI. They want to sell this software around the world.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and large banks — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: After shocking quarter, IBM insists that AI isn’t killing the mainframe
stories.simple_summary: IBM's sales dropped because companies are spending money on AI instead of the old computers IBM sells. IBM says this is temporary.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Google justifies its massive AI spending with a booming cloud business
stories.simple_summary: Google is making record profits by selling AI services to other companies that want to use AI. Their customers are paying well for this.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Treasury threatens sanctions after White House claims Moonshot distilled Anthropic’s Fable
stories.simple_summary: The U.S. government is threatening to punish companies involved in a dispute about copying AI software from one company to another. China's AI is involved in the debate.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a complex government and tech company issue — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: How OpenAI’s human mistake led to the AI-powered hack on Hugging Face
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI made a mistake when setting up a test area for AI. This mistake let hackers use AI to attack another company called Hugging Face.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a warning that even big tech companies make security mistakes — if you use cloud services, keep your passwords strong.

stories.title: Travis Kalanick’s robotics company raises $1.7B, led by a16z
stories.simple_summary: A company called Atoms that builds robots just got $1.7 billion in funding from investors. The company's founder used to run Uber.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Yope raises $12.3M to build a private social network without algorithms or ads
stories.simple_summary: A new social network app called Yope raised $12.3 million. It focuses on small groups of friends and family instead of showing ads or pushing content to many people.
stories.why_it_matters: If you want a social network without ads and algorithms picking what you see, this could be an option to watch for — but it's brand new.

stories.title: Monday.com lays off hundreds to focus on AI
stories.simple_summary: Monday.com, a company that makes work management software, is cutting 20% of its staff to focus on adding more AI features to its product.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Monday.com for your business, the product will likely get more AI tools — this shouldn't hurt your work.

stories.title: Here’s what Samsung’s smart glasses actually look like
stories.simple_summary: Samsung is making smart glasses that you can wear like regular glasses. They have a battery that lasts 9 hours. They're coming this fall.
stories.why_it_matters: These are a new gadget to consider if you want wearable technology — but they're expensive and not essential for most people.

stories.title: Arcee, a US open source AI lab, says Chinese models are not inherently dangerous
stories.simple_summary: A U.S. AI company says Chinese AI software isn't dangerous just because it comes from China. People are debating what rules should apply to Chinese AI.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a government policy debate between the U.S. and China — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Substack’s new tool tells you who’s been writing their newsletters with AI
stories.simple_summary: Substack, a newsletter platform, is adding a tool that shows readers whether the writer used AI to help write the newsletter. It helps with honesty.
stories.why_it_matters: If you read newsletters, you'll be able to see which ones had AI help — useful for knowing what you're reading.

stories.title: AMD commits up to $5 billion to  Anthropic
stories.simple_summary: AMD, a computer chip maker, is investing up to $5 billion in Anthropic, an AI company. AMD will also sell Anthropic special AI chips.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and tech companies — nothing you need to act on.
